seoTitle: How to Enter Your IRS IP PIN in TurboTax
metaDescription: Learn how to find and enter your IRS Identity Protection PIN in TurboTax so your return files securely and isn't rejected for a missing PIN.
intro: If the IRS has issued you an Identity Protection PIN, entering it correctly in TurboTax is a required step to keep your return secure and prevent tax fraud. This guide covers finding your IP PIN, entering it in TurboTax, and understanding why it matters for your filing. It's especially relevant for anyone who has experienced identity theft or was proactively issued a PIN for protection.

steps.heading: Find your IRS IP PIN
steps.body: Locate the Identity Protection PIN issued to you by the IRS before starting this step in TurboTax.

steps.heading: Enter the PIN in TurboTax
steps.body: Navigate to the identity protection section of your return and input the PIN exactly as issued.

steps.heading: Understand why the PIN matters
steps.body: The IP PIN adds a layer of security to your return, helping prevent someone else from filing a fraudulent return using your information.

steps.heading: Review other TurboTax security features
steps.body: Consider what other tools TurboTax offers for efficient, secure filing, especially if you're managing multiple returns as a professional.

faq.question: What happens if I don't enter my IP PIN?
faq.answer: A missing IP PIN when one is required can cause your e-filed return to be rejected, so it's an important step if the IRS has issued you one.

faq.question: Where do I get my IRS IP PIN if I've lost it?
faq.answer: The IRS provides ways to retrieve your IP PIN if you've misplaced it; check the IRS's official identity protection resources for retrieval options.

faq.question: Does everyone need an IP PIN to file in TurboTax?
faq.answer: No, only taxpayers who have been issued one by the IRS — often due to a history of identity theft or by opting in — need to enter it.

faq.question: Can tax professionals enter an IP PIN for a client?
faq.answer: Yes, this process applies whether you're filing your own return or a professional is entering the PIN on behalf of a client in TurboTax.
